$comment: RBAC catalog-grant audit map. The lint script (scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js) reads this file to know which requireXxx helper and which preHandler: requirePerm(...) route maps to which permission key. The original Wave 1 allow-list is auto-extracted from git show 0da6676:server/app.js. Any helper marked 'unmappable' has no static role list in the original (e.g. requireMfaPrivilegedUser calls mfaRequiredForRole) and needs a manual annotation block in the form: // rbac-audit: expected-roles Owner, Admin, Auditor inside the helper body. The script honors that annotation as the override.

generatedFrom.wave3AppJs: git show f2fd3c3:server/app.js (reverted migration that the audit would have caught)

requireOwner.note: Owner escape hatch — system.tenant.create is critical and Owner-only via the implicit-all shortcut. Legacy check was `user.role !== "Owner"` (single-role allow list).

requirePeopleWriter.note: hr.employee.create held by HROperator, PIIEditor (HR path) and FinanceOperator (Accountant path). Legacy: Owner, Admin, Accountant.

requireCrmEditor.note: Legacy allow-list uses the old role names 'Salesperson' / 'Service Manager' which were renamed to the current sales + service set (SalesLead, SalesManager, SalesRep, ServiceManager). The inline // rbac-audit: expected-roles annotation above the helper declares the current set: Owner, Admin, Operator, SalesLead, SalesManager, SalesRep, ServiceManager. The catalog grant of crm.deal.create is delivered via the new DealCreator perm set held by exactly that set.

requireCollectionEditor.note: Legacy allow-list uses the old role names 'Salesperson' / 'Service Manager' which were renamed to the current sales + service set (SalesLead, SalesManager, SalesRep, ServiceManager). The inline // rbac-audit: expected-roles annotation above the helper declares the current set: Owner, Admin, Operator, SalesLead, SalesManager, SalesRep, ServiceManager, Accountant. The catalog grant of crm.quote.send is delivered via the new QuoteSender perm set held by exactly that set.

requireFinanceOperator.note: Legacy: Owner, Admin, Accountant, Bookkeeper, FinanceLead, etc.

requireAnalyticsReportReader.note: Legacy: visibleAnalyticsReportTypes(user) — compound check, manually annotated below.

unmappable.$comment: Helpers that the original Wave 1 code gated on a non-static predicate (mfaRequiredForRole, canAccessInvoiceOverdueExplanation, etc.). The audit reports these as 'NO LEGACY ALLOW-LIST — needs manual annotation block in the helper body' and skips the subset check. To make one auditable, add a single-line JSDoc-style comment in the helper body in the form: `// rbac-audit: expected-roles Owner, Admin, Auditor` and the lint will pick it up automatically.
unmappable.requireMfaPrivilegedUser: uses mfaRequiredForRole(user.role) — MFA chain predicate, not a static list
unmappable.requireProductionReadinessReader: compound visibility check on the report list
unmappable.requireIntegrationReader: legacy: Owner, Admin, Auditor (will be auto-annotated)
unmappable.requireDocsWriter: legacy: Owner, Admin, Operator, Salesperson, Service Manager (long business list)
unmappable.requireDocsSigner: legacy: Owner, Admin, Operator, Salesperson, Service Manager
unmappable.requireProjectsWriter: legacy: Owner, Admin, ProjectLead, ProjectManager, ProjectMember

routes.$comment: Routes the Wave 3 workers successfully converted to preHandler: requirePerm(...). For each, the audit looks up the original in-handler check in the legacy 0da6676 app.js (parsed from git) and confirms the catalog grant is a subset of the legacy role set. The 6 routes below are the auth/security slice that was the only Wave 3 work considered clean enough to re-apply as-is in Wave 4's migrate-preHandlers-only worker.

auth-security-slice.legacyNote: uses mfaRequiredForRole(user.role) — annotated inline as Owner, Admin.
uses mfaRequiredForRole(user.role) — annotated inline as Owner, Admin.

catalog-inventory-purchase-pilots-slice.$comment: Routes from wave 3 commits a3e5d95 + eeb0f83. Not re-applied in wave 4; listed here so the catalog audit proves the grants today are correct. legacyAllowList is the inline-annotated expected-roles from the in-handler requireXxx(user) helper body, picked up by parseAllowListFromHelperBody. Routes without legacyAllowList are reported as 'no-legacy' so a future wave can fill in the annotation.

Auditor + FinanceLead + InventoryLead + PurchaseLead + Purchaser + WarehouseClerk hold the perm via the broad InventoryOperator/ReadOnly sets and are kept in the allow-list per the Wave 7 plan.

Accountant + FinanceLead + InventoryLead + PurchaseLead + Purchaser + WarehouseClerk hold the perm via the broad InventoryOperator set and are kept in the allow-list per the Wave 7 plan.

Wave 7 — narrow StockReader perm set created. Legacy allow-list rewritten to current role names. SalesLead/SalesManager/SalesRep + FinanceLead + InventoryLead + PurchaseLead + Purchaser + WarehouseClerk hold the perm via the broad InventoryOperator set and are kept in the allow-list per the Wave 7 plan. ServiceManager is NOT in the legacy allow-list for inventory/stock and is excluded.

Wave 7 — narrow StockReader perm set created. See /api/inventory/locations for the full note.

Wave 7 — narrow StockReceiver perm set created. Legacy allow-list rewritten to current role names. SalesLead/SalesManager/SalesRep + FinanceLead + InventoryLead + PurchaseLead + Purchaser + WarehouseClerk hold the perm via the broad InventoryOperator set and are kept in the allow-list per the Wave 7 plan. ServiceManager and Auditor are NOT in the legacy allow-list for stock.receive and are excluded.
